stories.story: Nine British citizens were arrested in Turkey on Wednesday, suspected of trying to cross illegally into Syria, the Turkish military said on its website. The nine British citizens were arrested at the Turkey-Syria border, the Turkish military said. The British Foreign Office said Wednesday that it is aware of reports of the arrests and that it is seeking information about the incident from Turkish authorities. The Turkish military did not say why the group of nine British citizens allegedly was trying to get into Syria. Syria has been torn by a roughly four-year war between Syrian government forces and Islamist extremist groups and other rebels.

stories.story: Nine British citizens were arrested in Turkey on Wednesday, suspected of trying to cross illegally into Syria, the Turkish military said on its website. The group of nine British citizens included four children -- the oldest being 10 or 11, with the youngest born in 2013, a Turkish official told CNN on condition of anonymity. Accompanying the four children were three men and two women; all nine British citizens had British passports, the Turkish official said. The nine British citizens were arrested at the Turkey-Syria border, the Turkish military said. The Turkish military did not say why the group of nine British citizens allegedly was trying to get into Syria.

stories.story: Syria has been torn by a roughly four-year war between Syrian government forces and Islamist extremist groups and other rebels. Among the war's combatants is ISIS, which has taken over parts of Syria and Iraq for what it claims is its Islamic caliphate, and which is known to have been recruiting Westerners. Nine British citizens were arrested in Turkey on Wednesday, suspected of trying to cross illegally into Syria, the Turkish military said on its website. The nine British citizens were arrested at the Turkey-Syria border, the Turkish military said. The Turkish military did not say why the group of nine British citizens allegedly was trying to get into Syria.
